title: telanthera frutescens var. manillensis
intro: Telanthera frutescens var. manillensis, also known as Red St. Augustine Grass or simply Red Grass, is a perennial plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of Telanthera frutescens var. manillensis can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, and are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other inflammatory disorders.
